identity_vector.description: Deterministic identity from a fixed 64-byte private key, and every value RNS derives from it. retinue must reproduce all of these exactly.

announce_plain.bin.description: RNS 1.3.8 announce for retinue.test. ratchets=off, app_data=none. Full packet including the 19-byte header.

announce_appdata.bin.description: RNS 1.3.8 announce for retinue.test. ratchets=off, app_data=yes. Full packet including the 19-byte header.

announce_ratchet.bin.description: RNS 1.3.8 announce for retinue.test. ratchets=on, app_data=none. Full packet including the 19-byte header.

announce_ratchet_appdata.bin.description: RNS 1.3.8 announce for retinue.test. ratchets=on, app_data=yes. Full packet including the 19-byte header.

announce_invalid_pubkey.bin.description: announce_appdata.bin with byte 24 XOR 0xFF, corrupting the pubkey. MUST fail validation.

announce_invalid_namehash.bin.description: announce_appdata.bin with byte 85 XOR 0xFF, corrupting the namehash. MUST fail validation.

announce_invalid_randhash.bin.description: announce_appdata.bin with byte 95 XOR 0xFF, corrupting the randhash. MUST fail validation.

announce_invalid_signature.bin.description: announce_appdata.bin with byte 108 XOR 0xFF, corrupting the signature. MUST fail validation.

announce_invalid_appdata.bin.description: announce_appdata.bin with byte 182 XOR 0xFF, corrupting the appdata. MUST fail validation.

announce_invalid_desthash.bin.description: announce_appdata.bin with byte 5 XOR 0xFF, corrupting the desthash. MUST fail validation.

token_identity.bin.description: Identity.encrypt() output for a known plaintext under the fixed identity. Layout: ephemeral_x25519_pub(32) || IV(16) || AES-256-CBC/PKCS7 ciphertext || HMAC-SHA256(32). Non-deterministic (random ephemeral key and IV), so retinue cannot byte-match it. retinue must DECRYPT it: that is the real test.

announce_layout.wire_payload: x25519_pub(32) || ed25519_pub(32) || name_hash(10) || rand_hash(10) || [ratchet(32) if context_flag] || signature(64) || app_data(*)
announce_layout.signed_message: dest_hash(16) || x25519_pub(32) || ed25519_pub(32) || name_hash(10) || rand_hash(10) || [ratchet(32)] || app_data(*)
announce_layout.signed_message_note: The signed message is the wire payload with the destination hash prepended and the signature spliced out. The destination hash is NOT in the payload: it comes from the packet header. Verified by independent Ed25519 verification against all four announce variants.

token.hmac_covers: IV || ciphertext  (the ephemeral public key is NOT authenticated)
token.verified_by: Independent decryption in scratch/crypto_probe.py using `cryptography` alone, trying all four (aes_size x split_order) combinations. Only AES-256 with sign-key-first both authenticates and decrypts.

provenance: Generated by oracle/capture.py against the RNS reference implementation, used strictly as a black box. RNS source was never read.
notes: A ratcheted announce is +32 bytes; the 32-byte ratchet public key is inserted between rand_hash and the signature (payload[84:116]), and its presence is signalled by bit 5 of header byte 0 (the Context Flag).

Beechat 0.1.0 models neither the context flag nor the ratchet, so it cannot parse or validate a ratcheted announce. Ratchets are off by default, which is the only reason a Beechat<->RNS pairing appears to work.
